note: Real-curated validation fixture. Held-out future evidence stays separate from event-time replay inputs.

rows.expected_observable: Future company evidence should show high claimant participation or settlement implementation progress.
rows.label: validated
rows.what_happened: Held-out 3M evidence said claimant participation was on track to exceed the 98% threshold and later filing language preserved the settlement structure, validating litigation overhang compression as the replay winner.

rows.expected_observable: Future validation should center PFAS or other liabilities rather than earplug settlement progress if this narrative wins.
rows.label: partial
rows.what_happened: Residual legal exposure remained relevant, but the held-out validation evidence directly centered Combat Arms participation and settlement implementation.

rows.expected_observable: The burden narrative should weaken if future company evidence confirms participation progress and cash election clarity.
rows.label: invalidated
rows.what_happened: 3M later said participation was on track to exceed the threshold and elected cash for the stock component, weakening the idea that settlement burden was the primary event narrative.

rows.expected_observable: Future validation should cite litigation posture rather than participation milestones if this narrative wins.
rows.label: pending
rows.what_happened: The no-liability posture remained part of the case file but did not become the central held-out validation evidence.
